seoTitle: How to Upload Multiple Files to Claude AI at Once
metaDescription: Learn how to batch upload multiple files to Claude AI so you can analyze reports, datasets, or documents together in one conversation.
intro: When you're working with several documents at once, uploading them one at a time slows you down. This guide covers how to batch upload multiple files to Claude AI so you can analyze or compare them together in a single conversation. It's useful for project managers, analysts, and anyone regularly handling multiple documents.

steps.heading: Prepare your files
steps.body: Gather the documents, reports, or datasets you want Claude AI to review, and make sure they're saved somewhere easy to access.

steps.heading: Access the upload feature
steps.body: Open a conversation in Claude AI and find the option to attach files to your message.

steps.heading: Select and upload multiple files at once
steps.body: Choose several files together rather than uploading and analyzing them one by one, which saves time when you need a combined view.

steps.heading: Ask Claude to work across all the files
steps.body: Once uploaded, prompt Claude to compare, summarize, or cross-reference the files together instead of treating each one separately.

steps.heading: Troubleshoot common upload issues
steps.body: If a file fails to upload, check its size and format, since very large files or unsupported types are the most common causes of upload problems.

faq.question: Is there a limit to how many files I can upload at once?
faq.answer: Claude AI does impose file count and size limits per conversation, so very large batches may need to be split across multiple uploads.

faq.question: What file types can I upload to Claude AI?
faq.answer: Claude AI generally supports common document and data formats like PDFs, text files, and spreadsheets, though exact support can vary.

faq.question: Can Claude compare data across multiple uploaded files?
faq.answer: Yes, once files are uploaded together, you can ask Claude to compare, summarize, or find patterns across all of them in the same conversation.

faq.question: Why did my file upload fail?
faq.answer: Failed uploads are usually caused by files that are too large or in an unsupported format, so check both before retrying.
